== Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato, Tokyo}}|shrine in {{jalink|Muroto|Q431889}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}|Mita Hachimangū}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=Mita Hachiman Shrine | 画像=[[File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_inner_shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 創建={{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{jalink|August 15|Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:need translation]]

Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:need translation]]

It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato, Tokyo}}|shrine in {{jalink|Muroto|Q431889}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}|Mita Hachimangū}} Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 創建={{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{jalink|August 15|Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:need translation]]

Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:need translation]]

== Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato, Tokyo}}|shrine in {{jalink|Muroto|Q431889}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}|Mita Hachimangū}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=Mita Hachiman Shrine | 画像=[[File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_inner_shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 創建={{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{jalink|August 15|Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:need translation]]

== Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato, Tokyo}}|shrine in {{jalink|Muroto|Q431889}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}|Mita Hachimangū}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=Mita Hachiman Shrine | 画像=[[File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_inner_shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 創建={{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{jalink|August 15|Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]]

In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato, Tokyo}}|shrine in {{jalink|Muroto|Q431889}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}|Mita Hachimangū}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=Mita Hachiman Shrine | 画像=[[File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_inner_shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 創建={{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{jalink|August 15|Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}}, {{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the {{jalink|Tōkaidō|Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{jalink|Edo Bay|Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{jalink|Shibaura|Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{jalink|Kamezuka Park|Q458122}}. In {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{jalink|Wadō|Q1373026}} 2 ({{jalink|709|Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{jalink|Kankō|Q2736722}} 7 ({{jalink|1011|Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{jalink|Saga Genji|Q11477318}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{jalink|Hieda Shrine|Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{jalink|Ōta|Q217234}}, and {{jalink|Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 2 ({{jalink|1662|Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{jalink|Isarago-zaka|Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{jalink|Keiō|Q776775}} 1 ({{jalink|1865|Q7708}}), the {{jalink|Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{jalink|1897|Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260|Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480|Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{jalink|Ontake Shrine|Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{jalink|Torii|Q234731|Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{jalink|Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|Minato}}{{jalink|Takanawa|Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
