{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたはちまんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]]

Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Mita Hachimangū}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=Mita Hachiman Shrine | 画像=[[File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_inner_shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]][[Category:qq]]

This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]]

2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|WD=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|WD=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|WD=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|WD=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|WD=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|WD=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|WD=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|WD=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|WD=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|WD=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|WD=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|WD=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|WD=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|WD=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|WD=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|WD=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|WD=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|WD=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|WD=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|qid=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|qid=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|qid=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|qid=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|qid=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|qid=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|qid=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|qid=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|qid=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|qid=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|qid=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|qid=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|qid=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|qid=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|qid=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|qid=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|qid=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|qid=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|qid=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|qid=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|qid=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|qid=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|qid=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|qid=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part); 2-chōme 1 (part), 16 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|qid=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} 3- and 4-chōme *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan (Minato, Tokyo)|Q11563057|qid=Q11563057|lt=Kōnan|1=Kōnan, Tokyo}} 1-5 chōme === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minato/3053/|title= Mita Hachiman Shrine [Hachiman-sama]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-07-26|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|qid=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|year=1927|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|author=Saitō Yukio|chapter=Ten-sū section: Mita Hachimangū|pages=261-264|id={{NDLJP|1174130/137}}}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Mita-Hachiman-jinja}} * [http://mitahachiman.net/ Official site of Mita Hachiman Shrine] {{coord|35|38|35|N|139|44|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mita Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:三田 (東京都港区)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|qid=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}3-7-16{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|35.3|N|139|44|29|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|qid=Q23514|1=709}}){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|qid=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Tokyo city|label=Mita
Hachiman Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mita Hachiman Shrine''' (みたはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|qid=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its principal deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachiman deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} as associated deities. It stands on a slope facing the sea side. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō|Q1196306|qid=Q1196306|1=Tōkaidō (region)}} ran right in front, and beyond it opened the shore of {{ill|lt=Edo Bay|Q11551101|qid=Q11551101|1=Edo Bay}}, likely giving scenic views. This changed when reclamation of {{ill|lt=Shibaura|Q13489497|qid=Q13489497|1=Shibaura}} began in the Meiji 40s. Tree pruning around the shrine is kept minimal, leaving a dim and quiet atmosphere. On the rear high ground is adjacent {{ill|lt=Kamezuka Park|Q458122|qid=Q458122|1=Kamezuka Park}}. In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 21, to commemorate 1300 years of enshrinement, a regular periodic grand festival was held on August 1 and a mikoshi procession on August 2. ===History=== In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|qid=Q23514|1=709}}), it began as a shrine of protection for the eastern provinces, enshrined at Makioka in Musashi. In {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|qid=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1011|Q19238|qid=Q19238|1=1011}}), it was moved to Kubo-Mita, Mita-gō, Musashi, and revered as clan shrine of the Watanabe branch of {{ill|lt=Saga Genji|Q11477318|qid=Q11477318|1=Saga Genji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it is recorded as "Hieda Shrine" in Ebara District, Musashi. (The "Engishiki Hieda Shrine" also has other candidate shrines, including {{ill|lt=Hieda Shrine|Q11596288|qid=Q11596288|1=Hieta Shrine}} in Kamata, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, and {{ill|lt=Rokugō Shrine|Q17215324|qid=Q17215324|1=Rokugō Shrine}} in Higashi-Rokugō.) When {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Edo Castle, an auspicious sign was said to have occurred, and he opened and developed the coast of Kami-Takanawa village (Ebara District) at the current site, building shrine structures there. Relocation was carried out in August {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|qid=Q7574|1=1662}}). However, a great fire in February Kanbun 8 burned all shrine buildings, and a temporary shrine was placed at Yakushi-ji on {{ill|lt=Isarago-zaka|Q11379690|qid=Q11379690|1=Isarago-zaka}}. Shrine buildings were rebuilt and relocated again in August Kanbun 12. In {{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|qid=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1865|Q7708|qid=Q7708|1=1865}}), the {{ill|lt=Jingikan (Meiji)|Q108166762|qid=Q108166762|lt=Jingikan|1=Jingi-kan}} renamed it "Hieda Shrine" as in Engishiki; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}) it was renamed "Mita Hachiman Shrine" and ranked gōsha; in {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|qid=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30) it reverted to '''Mita Hachiman Shrine'''{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. Shrine buildings from Kanbun 12 were burned in the 1945 Tokyo air raids and rebuilt postwar in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 29. ===Structures=== * Shrine complex - honden, heiden, haiden * Precinct shrines - Gokō Inari Shrine, Ontake Shrine * Precinct land - 2,975.9 m² * Precinct buildings - kaguraden, temizuya, shamusho === Gallery === File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Kaguraden.JPG|{{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_Chōzuya.JPG|Purification basin File:Ontake_Shrine.jpg|{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|lt=Gokō Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} / {{ill|lt=Ontake Shrine|Q11488964|qid=Q11488964|1=Mitake shrine}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_torii.JPG|{{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|lt=Great torii|1=Torii}} File:Mita-Hachiman-jinja_0240.JPG|Stone steps === Ujiko area === *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Mita (Minato, Tokyo)|Q3317048|qid=Q3317048|lt=Mita|1=Mita, Minato, Tokyo}} 3-chōme 5-14, 4-chōme 9 and 18-19 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Shiba (Minato, Tokyo)|Q2631862|qid=Q2631862|lt=Shiba|1=Shiba, Tokyo}} 5-chōme 29-36 *{{ill|lt=Minato, Tokyo|Q190088|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Takanawa|Q2749804|qid=Q2749804|1=Takanawa}} 1-chōme 4 (part), 5 (part), 6-15, 16 (part);
